title: Rajma Rice Bowl
description: Hearty North Indian kidney-bean curry over rice. High fiber, high satisfaction, meal-prep friendly.
explanation: Rajma-chawal is a complete comfort plate: beans for protein and fiber, rice for energy, onion-tomato masala for depth. This version is fully plant-based with no cream or ghee.
Soaking beans overnight is ideal. Canned or pre-boiled rajma works for weeknights.
ingredients: 1 cup Dried Rajma (kidney beans), soaked overnight — or 2.5 cups boiled
Coriander and onion rings to garnish
instructions: Pressure-cook soaked rajma with water and salt until soft (usually 4–5 whistles).
Heat oil, crackle cumin, saute onion until golden.
Add ginger-garlic; cook until raw smell goes. Add tomato and cook until oil separates.
Add turmeric, coriander, chilli powder, and salt. Cook 1–2 minutes.
Add boiled rajma with enough cooking liquid for a thick gravy. Simmer 10–15 minutes.
Finish with garam masala and coriander. Serve over rice.
stepNotes: Undercooked beans ruin the dish—beans should mash easily between fingers.
Mash a few beans into the gravy for restaurant-style body without cream.
Kashmiri chilli gives color with moderate heat.
A longer simmer deepens flavor more than extra oil.
Day-old rajma often tastes better after flavors settle.

variationSections.title: Diet adjustments
items: Lower calorie: use less oil and more gravy liquid; serve with less rice and more salad.
Higher protein: add extra beans or a side of tofu.
No-onion/no-garlic: use hing, ginger, tomato, and kasuri methi.

variationSections.title: Bowl toppings
items: Lemon, raw onion, cucumber salad, or pickled chilli.
Brown rice or millets instead of white rice.
A spoon of peanut curd on the side.
